title: comarum palustre subsp. arcticum
intro: Comarum palustre subsp. arcticum, also known as Arctic bog bilberry or marsh bilberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its small, blue-black berries and fragrant, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help with wound healing and reducing swelling. The medicinal properties of Comarum palustre subsp. arcticum have been attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds, which may provide antioxidant and anti-cancer benefits, as well as support cardiovascular health and immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
